preserved_from_EXP_255: Eclipse "Live test pending" badge linking to #p4-426 (verified stable)
Body-weight figures row preserving the same four headline figures
prefers-reduced-motion override on the badge dot pulse
Item 2 audit (no-op finding) and chunking of Items 3 + 4 to follow-up EXPs
fixed_in_EXP_261: CRITICAL H1: FC1 find anchor now uses ds-scorecard / ds-sc-hero / ds-sc-card / ds-sc-number / ds-sc-label / ds-sc-sublabel (verified by grep — count of "ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" in build-scripts/generate-html.js = 1; count without ds- prefix = 0).
CRITICAL H1: FC2 find anchor now uses .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars (verified — both ds- prefixed strings exist exactly once at lines 550-551).

CRITICAL H2: FC1 verification gate replaced. New post-condition: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js  # expect 0 (only after patch). Paired with positive grep 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' (expect >= 1). The negative gate is no longer tautological — it queries a string that exists pre-patch and only goes to 0 post-patch.

MODERATE H3: All NEW classes prefixed with ds-: ds-thesis-hero, ds-thesis-statement, ds-thesis-meta, ds-thesis-badge, ds-thesis-badge-dot, ds-thesis-badge-pre, ds-thesis-badge-date, ds-thesis-figures, ds-tf-item, ds-tf-number, ds-tf-textnum, ds-tf-accent, ds-tf-label, ds-tf-link. Keyframe renamed to ds-thesis-badge-pulse. id="thesis-statement" renamed to id="ds-thesis-statement" (and matching aria-labelledby). Total: 14 classes + 1 keyframe + 1 id.

MODERATE H4: load_bearing_content_audit corrected. The literal phrase "zero falsifications" is acknowledged as removed from the Overview tab (it existed only in the deleted sublabel; the accuracy-math-section directly below uses different phrasing). Rhetorical work is taken up by the figures row "Survive scrutiny: 0", which conveys the same conclusion at body weight. This is now stated explicitly rather than denied.
not_fixed_in_EXP_261_with_rationale: MINOR H5 (above-the-fold estimate): The proposal text estimate is updated to use the curmudgeon's re-derived numbers (thesis ~200px on 1440x900, badge meta ~36px, figures row ~90px → ~330px hero, ~450px including ~120px title block above; mobile <420px hero ~360px). This is a documentation-only change in the curmudgeon_review_focus and acceptance_criteria fields. No code change needed.

MINOR H6 (dark-mode pulse hardcodes light-mode rgba): NOT FIXED in this EXP per HNOTE constraint "Just fix the class names and find anchors." The dark-mode pulse-ring color stays light-mode-blue (rgba(74,111,165,.45)) in dark mode. Recommended follow-up EXP: introduce --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb CSS custom property (74,111,165 in :root, 143,175,212 in @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) :root) and reference via rgba(var(--ds-thesis-pulse-rgb),.45). Curmudgeon flagged this as "minor" and "does not block".

MINOR H7 (Wrong side textnum asymmetry): NOT FIXED in this EXP per HNOTE constraint and per curmudgeon "polish call, does not block". The figures row preserves the four-position layout the operator specified; the .ds-tf-textnum class handles the textual fourth figure. Recommended follow-up EXP: replace "Wrong side" with a date string ("Apr 7" or "Apr 2026") to retire the textnum class and make all four figures share .ds-tf-number style. Operator decision required because it changes user-visible copy.

hero_swap_design.layout_principle: Thesis at display scale → supporting figures at body scale. Same as EXP-255; preserved verbatim per HNOTE.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_verbatim: None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.
hero_swap_design.thesis_sentence_provenance: Operator-authored in HNOTE-OPERATOR-DESIGN-REVIEW-V2-OVERVIEW-REWORK-001 (created 2026-04-25T21:54:32Z). Wording preserved verbatim except literal "69" parameterized to ${counts.total} per project rule against hardcoded derived numbers (CLAUDE.md). Today live count = 71.
hero_swap_design.thesis_treatment: clamp(1.85rem, 3.6vw, 2.65rem) serif heading-color, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.012em, line-height 1.18, max-width 32ch with text-wrap:balance. Re-derived fold check on 1440x900 desktop: thesis ~200px (4 lines @ ~50px line-height), meta row ~36px, figures row ~90px → ~330px hero. Adding ~120px title block above gives ~450px first-screen footprint, well below a 900px fold. Mobile <420px hero ~360px, still above iPhone fold but consumes more of the first viewport.
hero_swap_design.badge_treatment: Pill chip, accent border-color, accent text-color, transparent background, with a small pulsing accent-color dot for liveness. Hover inverts to filled accent. prefers-reduced-motion disables the pulse. Links to #p4-426 (section 4.2.6 "What would change our verdict") and switches to the "pages" tab.
hero_swap_design.badge_link_target_rationale: Section 4.2.6 already exists today (verified: id="p4-426" in built docs/index.html, heading "4.2.6 What would change our verdict"). EXP-254 has integrated; 4.2.6 was not renumbered. Badge link will not break.
hero_swap_design.figures_treatment: Four-column grid below the thesis, separated by a 1px rule-color top border. Each item: serif 1.55rem number + sans-serif 0.78rem uppercase letterspaced label + (where applicable) a small Details link. Mobile breakpoints at 760px (2 cols) and 420px (1 col).
hero_swap_design.accessibility: ds-thesis-statement gets id="ds-thesis-statement" used by section aria-labelledby. Badge gets descriptive aria-label naming the date and target section. Figures list uses role="list" with aria-label="Headline figures". Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion. Color contrast for accent text on transparent and white-on-accent on hover both audit clean against WCAG AA in the existing palette (--accent #4A6FA5 on white background = 4.65:1; white on --accent = 4.65:1).

load_bearing_content_audit_v2.purpose: Per curmudgeon H4: the EXP-255 audit incorrectly claimed "zero falsifications" was preserved in accuracy-math-section. Curmudgeon verified: phrase exists exactly once in generate-html.js, in the deleted sublabel; accuracy-math-section uses different phrasing.
load_bearing_content_audit_v2.correction: Accept the loss explicitly. The phrase "zero falsifications" was a rhetorical compression of two adjacent claims — the dome's asserted accuracy (which we do not concede) and the dome's admitted falsification count of zero (which we contradict numerically). After the swap, both claims remain on the Overview through the figures row: "Claimed wins: ${counts.total}" + "Survive scrutiny: 0" + "Acknowledged + silent failures: ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures}". The third figure numerically contradicts any "zero falsifications" framing more directly than the prose did. The exact two-word phrase is gone; the rhetorical work is done by the data row.

items.original: The author claims ${counts.total} confirmed predictions and zero falsifications
items.in_new_hero: PARTIALLY PRESERVED. The "${counts.total} confirmed" half is implicit in "Claimed wins: ${counts.total}". The "zero falsifications" half is GONE from this Overview position. The accuracy-math-section directly below the new hero contains the numerical contradiction ("the dome's public denominator includes only ${failures.dome_claimed_failures} acknowledged falsifications") which carries the same load at body weight. The compact rhetorical phrase is lost; the rhetorical work is preserved.

items.original: Not one claim produces a result that the globe model can't explain and the dome uniquely can
items.in_new_hero: FULLY PRESERVED at hero scale. The new thesis sentence ("None of the ${counts.total} predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain") is the same idea promoted from body-tile sublabel to display-scale heading. Net: stronger, not weaker.

items.original: He admits ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}. We found ${silentFailures} more he silently removed.
items.in_new_hero: NUMBERS PRESERVED in figures row as ${failures.dome_claimed_failures}+${silentFailures}, with Details link to #p3-failures. The "he admits / silently removed" prose framing is in accuracy-math-section directly below (untouched by this patch).

items.original: His blockchain proof timestamps the observations, not the predictions. As of April 2026, the author concedes this distinction in writing — but hasn't fixed the underlying proof structure.
items.in_new_hero: HEADLINE PRESERVED ("Wrong side" + "Timestamp error" + Details link). The full prose lives in the dedicated Timestamp Error tab and the Overview's Breaking News block (both untouched). No load-bearing content lost.

load_bearing_content_audit_v2.conclusion: All load-bearing CONTENT remains accessible from the Overview within one scroll or one tab-click. One specific PHRASE ("zero falsifications") is removed from the Overview tab — the curmudgeon's H4 is correct on this point. The figures row's numerical contradiction is rhetorically equivalent and arguably stronger because it shows the contradiction with our numbers rather than restating the dome's framing.

kernel_of_truth.description: The thesis-led composition (per EXP-255 kernel) IS rhetorically sound. The accessibility plumbing is competently done. The Item 2 audit is correctly resolved as no-op. The chunking choice (Items 1+2+5 in this EXP, Items 3+4 deferred) is permitted by the HNOTE and minimizes integration risk. The badge link target is verifiably stable. All of these survive the rework — none of the curmudgeon's holes touched the design substance.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim_v1: EXP-255 (v1) was right on substance and wrong on execution. A patch that does not match its targets does no work. The decider would have caught it via the positive grep gate (class="thesis-hero" expected ≥ 1) — that gate would fail post-build because nothing was actually inserted — but only if the decider runs the FULL acceptance check rather than relying on the negative gate. The negative gate was tautological: grep for a string that does not exist, get 0, declare success. That hides the bug.
kernel_of_truth.why_v2_saves_it: EXP-261 (this rework) addresses execution without altering substance: (a) FC1+FC2 anchors corrected to actual ds- prefixed strings, verified pre-patch by grep -c = 1; (b) FC1 negative gate changed to grep "ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" expect 0 (only goes to 0 after patch — meaningful); (c) all new classes prefixed ds- to match design-system invariant; (d) load-bearing audit corrected to acknowledge the "zero falsifications" phrase loss; (e) thesis sentence and figures row preserved verbatim. The substance survives the rework intact.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not also fix the dark-mode pulse rgba (curmudgeon H6) in the same EXP? It's only four lines.
anticipated_objections.response: HNOTE-DECIDER-EXP261-EXP255-REWORK-20260426 explicitly scopes this rework to "fix the class names and find anchors. Just." The curmudgeon flagged H6 as minor and does-not-block. Adding a CSS custom property for pulse color would require two additional find/replace pairs (one to inject --ds-thesis-pulse-rgb into :root at line 512, one for the @media(prefers-color-scheme:dark) :root block at line 513), expanding the patch surface and risking new anchor mismatches. Cleaner to ship the rework with the same cosmetic regression EXP-255 had, then file a follow-up EXP for the dark-mode pulse if anyone notices in dark mode. Curmudgeon's spot-check verdict is gated on critical+moderate fixes only.

anticipated_objections.objection: The decider should fail-loud on exact_string_replace miss anyway. Why care about the verification gate?
anticipated_objections.response: Curmudgeon already flagged this as a structural recommendation for tinker — the integration pipeline's exact_string_replace miss handling. That's out of scope for an analyst expansion. What we CAN fix here is the verification gate itself: even if the integration pipeline silently swallows a miss, the post-condition grep should NOT pass when the patch fails. The new gate (grep "ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero" expect 0) only goes to 0 after a successful patch — if the patch fails, the count stays at 1 and the gate fails. This is independent of any pipeline-level fix and is the right defense-in-depth.

anticipated_objections.objection: Renaming the id from thesis-statement to ds-thesis-statement breaks any external links to #thesis-statement.
anticipated_objections.response: The id was newly introduced by EXP-255 and EXP-255 is not yet integrated. There are no external links to it (verified: grep -r "thesis-statement" docs/ before this rework returns 0 matches because the EXP-255 patch was rejected before integration). The id is internal scaffolding for aria-labelledby; renaming both sides of that pair (id + aria-labelledby) preserves the accessibility relationship. Net: no breakage because the id never landed.

anticipated_objections.objection: The figures row keeps the .ds-tf-textnum asymmetry the curmudgeon flagged in H7.
anticipated_objections.response: Yes. H7 is "polish, does not block" per curmudgeon and "preserve the figures row layout" per HNOTE. The textnum class lets the operator say "Wrong side" rather than a date — that is the operator's explicit copy choice. A follow-up EXP could replace it with a date string and retire the class, but that requires operator approval because it changes user-visible content. Out of scope for this rework.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not parameterize the rgba color values in the keyframe via CSS custom properties anyway, since you're already touching the CSS rules?
anticipated_objections.response: See the first objection. The HNOTE scopes this to anchor + class-name fixes. CSS-property refactors are deferred to a follow-up. Adding properties also changes :root, which is a separate concern from .ds-thesis-hero scope.

acceptance_criteria: build-scripts/generate-html.js no longer contains class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero".
build-scripts/generate-html.js CSS const contains the .ds-thesis-hero rule between .ds-sc-domains and .ds-verdict-bars (preserving the existing rule order).
build-scripts/generate-html.js still contains the .ds-verdict-bars{margin:1.5rem 0 2rem;padding:0;border:none} rule (FC2 preserves it as the trailing line of the replacement).

No new class without ds- prefix introduced (verified: grep for orphan class names — thesis-hero, thesis-statement, thesis-meta, thesis-badge, thesis-figures, tf-item, tf-number, tf-label, tf-link, tf-accent, tf-textnum — should all return 0 hits after the patch).
node build.js html succeeds without warnings.

Built docs/index.html contains the rendered thesis sentence with substituted ${counts.total} value (today: 71) — i.e., grep "None of the 71 predictions produces a dome-specific result" docs/index.html  # expect ≥ 1.
Built docs/index.html contains the badge with href="#p4-426" and onclick="showTab('pages');return false".
Built docs/index.html contains all four ds-tf-item figures (claimed wins / survive scrutiny / failures / timestamp error) with their numbers and Details links.
node test.js passes all existing assertions.

Visual check (curmudgeon spot-check post-integration): thesis sentence at clamp(1.85rem,3.6vw,2.65rem) computed size on 1440x900; badge below thesis at ~0.82rem; figures row at body weight; nothing exceeds the fold (re-derived estimate: ~330px hero + ~120px title block = ~450px first-screen, comfortably below 900px).
curmudgeon_review_focus: Spot-check that the FC1 find anchor matches the live file exactly: grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js should return 1 BEFORE the patch is applied. If 0, the rework is still wrong and must bounce back again.

Spot-check that the FC2 find anchor matches: grep -c '\.ds-sc-domains{grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr)}' should return 1, AND grep -c '\.ds-verdict-bars{margin:1\.5rem 0 2rem;padding:0;border:none}' should return 1. Both anchor lines exist as line 550 and 551 respectively.

Verify the FC1 negative gate is no longer tautological: BEFORE patch, grep 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' = 1 (not 0). AFTER patch, that grep = 0. The gate now distinguishes patched-vs-unpatched state.

Verify all new classes carry the ds- prefix. Spot-check by grep: thesis-hero, thesis-statement, thesis-meta, thesis-badge, thesis-figures, tf-item, tf-number, tf-label, tf-link, tf-accent, tf-textnum, thesis-badge-pulse — none should appear unprefixed in the final patched file. (The keyframe is renamed to ds-thesis-badge-pulse and referenced as such.)
Verify the load_bearing_content_audit_v2 explicitly accepts the "zero falsifications" phrase loss rather than denying it (curmudgeon H4).
Out of scope for spot-check: H5 fold estimate (text-only correction), H6 dark-mode pulse color (deferred per HNOTE), H7 textnum asymmetry (deferred per HNOTE).
Out of scope: full content-preservation audit (already done in EXP-255 c1).
Recommendation: if the four spot-checks above pass, this is ready for integration. No need for a full curmudgeon cycle.

decider_instructions.integration_step_1: Apply FC1 (HTML hero replacement) via exact_string_replace in build-scripts/generate-html.js. Verify with: grep -c 'class="ds-thesis-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c 'class="ds-scorecard ds-sc-hero"' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 0).
decider_instructions.integration_step_2: Apply FC2 (CSS rule insertion) via exact_string_replace. Verify with: grep -c '\.ds-thesis-hero{' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c '@keyframes ds-thesis-badge-pulse' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1) AND grep -c '\.ds-verdict-bars{margin:1\.5rem 0 2rem;padding:0;border:none}' build-scripts/generate-html.js (expect 1, preserved).
decider_instructions.integration_step_3: If either exact_string_replace returns "no match", STOP. Do NOT proceed to build. Bounce back to analyst with the failed find string. (This is defense in depth: the analyst-side anchor verification should have caught any drift, but the decider should fail-loud just in case.)
decider_instructions.integration_step_4: Run build: node build.js html. Build must succeed without warnings.
decider_instructions.integration_step_5: Verify rendered HTML: grep -c 'ds-thesis-statement' docs/index.html (expect ≥ 1); grep -c 'ds-thesis-badge' docs/index.html (expect ≥ 1).
decider_instructions.integration_step_6: Run tests: node test.js. All existing assertions should still pass. (No test.js patches in this EXP.)
decider_instructions.routing: queue-for-curmudgeon-spot-check-then-integrate per HNOTE explicit directive. Curmudgeon recommends spot-check (single-pass verification) rather than full re-review since substance is unchanged.

build_impact.additive_lines_estimate: ~24 CSS rule lines + ~22 HTML template lines = ~46 lines added (same as EXP-255).
build_impact.net_delta_lines: +~24 lines (slightly less than EXP-255 because four-up block is one line longer with ds- prefix).
build_impact.file_size_change_estimate: <2.5 KB net increase in generate-html.js (small bump from ds- prefix on each new class); <2.5 KB net increase in built docs/index.html.
build_impact.pdf_impact: PDF unchanged from EXP-255 analysis: Playwright headless Chromium renders all standard properties; animation does not appear in static PDF, which is correct.

out_of_scope_explicit: EXP-255 Items 3 (lightweight 4-row falsification module) and 4 (How-to-Evaluate 5-question preface) — still deferred to follow-up EXPs as in EXP-255.
EXP-190 (full Scoreboard with new schema) — still parked.
Verdict bar chart, accuracy-math-section, Verdict Categories block, title-block above hero — all preserved untouched (same as EXP-255).
Curmudgeon H6 (dark-mode pulse rgba) — deferred to follow-up EXP per HNOTE constraint.
Curmudgeon H7 (Wrong side textnum asymmetry) — deferred per HNOTE constraint and per curmudgeon "polish, does not block".
Operator copy changes (e.g., replacing "Wrong side" with a date) — requires operator approval.
newsworthy: Document goes lead-with-thesis (final). Overview now opens with "None of the 71 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain" at hero scale. Same change EXP-255 attempted, but with patch anchors that actually match the codebase.
notes_for_decider: (1) Two find/replace pairs in build-scripts/generate-html.js — both anchors verified by grep -c = 1 in this clean clone before writing this expansion. (2) The decider should treat exact_string_replace miss as a HARD FAILURE, not advisory. If either find returns "no match", bounce back to analyst rather than proceeding to build. (3) Per HNOTE explicit directive, route to curmudgeon for SPOT-CHECK (single-pass) review BEFORE integrating to main. (4) After spot-check clearance, integrate. The four critical+moderate curmudgeon holes are addressed; the two minor ones are deferred per HNOTE scope. (5) HNOTE-DECIDER-EXP261-EXP255-REWORK-20260426 should be marked consumed by this EXP. (6) The original EXP-255 file (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-255-overview-hero-rework.json) should be marked superseded — leave the file in place (append-only directory) but the tracker entry for EXP-255 transitions to "superseded-by-EXP-261".
